name: v8.0.0
body:
## v8.0.0 — January 10, 2026

This is a major release with significant API improvements. Please read the migration guide before upgrading.

## Breaking Changes

- `stripe.webhooks.constructEvent()` has been renamed to `stripe.webhooks.verify()`. The old method has been removed entirely. Update all webhook handlers to use the new method.
- `createCustomer()` now requires the `email` parameter. Previously this was optional; it is now mandatory to improve deliverability of Stripe-generated receipts.
- `stripe.charges.create()` is removed — use `stripe.paymentIntents.create()` instead. The Charges API is fully deprecated.
- `StripeError.type` property removed — use `StripeError.code` instead for error classification.
- Minimum Node.js version bumped from 12 to 18. Node 12, 14, and 16 are no longer supported.

## Migration Guide

### Webhook verification

Before:
```js
const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(payload, sig, secret);
```

After:
```js
const event = await stripe.webhooks.verify(payload, sig, secret);
```

Note: `verify()` is now async and returns a Promise. Ensure your handler is `async`.

### Customer creation

Before:
```js
const customer = await stripe.customers.create({ name: 'Jenny Rosen' });
```

After:
```js
const customer = await stripe.customers.create({
  name: 'Jenny Rosen',
  email: 'jenny@example.com'  // now required
});
```

## New Features

- `stripe.webhooks.verify()` now supports Ed25519 signature verification
- Improved TypeScript types across all resources
- Added `stripe.paymentLinks.create()` convenience method
- `stripe.customers.search()` now supports cursor-based pagination

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed race condition in webhook signature timing validation
- Resolved memory leak in long-running server processes
- Corrected TypeScript overload for `stripe.invoices.pay()`

## Deprecations (will be removed in v9)

- `stripe.customers.update()` — use `stripe.customers.modify()` (alias with clearer name)
- `autoRetry` option — use `maxNetworkRetries` instead

For a complete migration guide, see [MIGRATION.md](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node/blob/main/MIGRATION.md).
